questions.question: Translate to FOL: 'Every student who takes an exam gets a grade'
expectedPoints: Identify predicates: Student(x), Takes(x,y), Exam(y), GetsGrade(x,z), Grade(z)
Use universal quantifiers for students and exams

questions.question: Explain the difference between syntax and semantics in First Order Logic, providing examples of each
expectedPoints: Syntax defines formal structure of formulas (rules for well-formedness)
Examples: ∀x P(x) is syntactically valid, P(x ∀ is not
Semantics gives meaning to formulas through interpretations
Example: Interpretation specifies domain and mappings for symbols
Syntax without semantics is meaningless; semantics without syntax is inexpressible
Both are necessary for a complete logical system
